highlights.text: This is irrelevant for us day traders but useful for swing traders (support down at 6913).” I also wrote on Friday at 4pm: “Bear case Monday/Tuesday: Bear case tomorrow: 6969 needs to fail.” Over the weekend, ES got slammed with more tariff headlines and ES sold right down to 6913 flag support on Sunday evening.
I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “Generally, in a strong bull case ES will hold 6822 OR quick trap down to 6810-13.
The first principle is that when ES sells it typically goes elevator down and violently flushes every support.
Then, ES short squeezes but this ONLY happens when ES sees a Failed Breakdown (my core setup).
The obvious entry to watch is the Failed Breakdown of 6847.
We trapped below 6822 (yesterdays daily low) down to 6814, recovered for the Failed Breakdown by 9am, and ran those targets to 6913+.
This showed by the power of my core setup, the Failed Breakdown.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

post_title: Bulls Bought The SPX Dip As Expected. Is The Bottom In? January 22nd Plan
